manuals.title: Technical Manuals
manuals.intro: "Below, we provide technical manuals supplied by third parties, with detailed information on the main equipment used in assembling your NOMAD catamaran. With this information, you can understand and operate each component accurately, ensuring the durability and performance of the installed equipment."

history.title: History of the Catamaran
history.p1: How the catamaran emerged: Catamarans originated in Micronesia and Polynesia out of a need to improve stability and speed, as an alternative to monohulls. It was through these vessels that these people occupied the Pacific islands (including the colonization of Hawaii). The first Westerners to come into contact with these vessels were the Spaniards, who were very impressed with the speed of this type of boat.
history.p2: How it developed: In the 1950s in England, the first modern sailing catamarans for competition were developed. The first generation of designers and builders of modern multihulls emerged with a growing variety of sailing and motor vessels for sport and leisure. Since then, with the development of materials and construction techniques, catamarans have become an option for various areas of naval engineering, with their most varied forms, whether conventional, symmetrical, or asymmetrical hulls.
history.p3: The catamaran today: With the development of materials and structural knowledge, catamarans are gaining more space in navigation every day, whether for passenger or cargo transport, as in the case of ferry boats, or for leisure, such as sailboats, racing boats, and cruisers. In the transport area, the catamaran has proven to be an excellent solution for short lines of up to 1000 km (540 miles), with speeds exceeding 30 knots, becoming an effective competitor to the already saturated means of transport in major production centers. The catamaran will be an option whenever economy, comfort, and stability are project requirements.

infusion.title: Construction Process: Vacuum Infusion
infusion.p1: What it is: Vacuum infusion is one of the most innovative processes in the nautical industry. It is a method that often ends up being more efficient and practical than manual lamination, for example. Each case is different; boats require specific construction processes and methods depending on their size and use.
infusion.p2: Where it applies: The infusion method is the process used for the construction of high-performance and quality boats, seen in the manufacture of boats by major shipyards around the world. It is an industrial-scale method, and thus, requires structure and experience to be performed.
infusion.p3: How it works: In the vacuum infusion process, all material, PVC foams, and fabrics are placed dry in the ready mold, and thus enveloped by a vacuum plastic bag. When the vacuum pump is activated, the air will leave the plastic and the resin will be transferred, through hoses connected to a resin bucket, into the fiber layers by the differential action of internal and external pressure.
infusion.p4: Mechanical characteristics: Laminates manufactured by the infusion process are more resistant than conventional laminates manufactured manually, as their impregnation between fibers reaches 60%, while in manual lamination it reaches only 20 to 30% of fiber content. With the infusion method, we leave aside environmental and unhealthiness problems, as there is no waste of material and no direct contact between the builder and the resins.
infusion.p5: Advantages of Infusion: Boat constructions through vacuum infusion invariably use the sandwich system, which means they will present greater thermal insulation, making it economical when thinking about the use of air conditioning on the boat and the energy produced by the generator system to power them. Another saving to be observed is that of resin and fibers. As the resin is not touched during lamination, only during infusion into the laminate, waste is avoided. In the case of fibers, the fact is that during manual lamination many fibers end up falling on the floor, or remain partially impregnated, without being able to be reused, whereas in the infusion method, as the lamination is done dry, the fiber can be pre-cut and adjusted on site with precision.
infusion.p6: Considerations: High-performance catamaran boats require a construction process that ensures high strength and low weight. Among the construction processes employed in the nautical industry, the vacuum infusion process proved to be the best option to ensure the highest quality standards.

hulls.title: Hull Types in Catamaran Boats
hulls.p1: Currently, there is a multitude of catamaran hull shapes. Project requirements such as speed, expected behavior, motorization, and costs will define which type of hull is most suitable. Catamaran boats generally fall into three main types listed below:
hulls.p2: Semi-displacement hulls: This type of hull has a more rounded bottom design, generating a profile more suitable for absorbing impact with waves. These types of hulls have a well-defined optimal speed range but are less efficient for high speeds.
hulls.p3: Symmetrical planing hulls: Planing hull catamarans are those that present predominantly flat hull sections. Geometry that promotes greater lift and facilitates the planing regime, being more suitable for cases where speed and low fuel consumption are project requirements.
hulls.p4: Asymmetrical planing hulls: This type of hull is dedicated to boats that will navigate at high speeds. Although it generally requires more powerful motorization, this type of hull gains efficiency at high speeds.

fishing.title: Fishing Boat, how to choose...
fishing.p1: In addition to appropriate techniques and equipment, the open sea fisherman needs a boat capable of taking him in the best, fastest, and most comfortable way to his favorite fishing spot. For this, it is necessary to use boats that meet minimum requirements for economy, navigability, safety, and autonomy. The correct choice guarantees much more pleasure for the fisherman and increases the chances of fish on the line. Mastro D'Ascia develops catamaran boats approved for open sea navigation that offer maximum economy and navigability.
fishing.p2: Bottom fishing: As the name implies, this type of fishing is carried out in deep places. It consists of placing the bait, preferably natural ones, at the bottom. The boat used needs space for circulation and working with the fish. Recommended model: NOMAD 7CC, TITAN CC.
fishing.p3: High sea or oceanic fishing: For this modality, boats with powerful engines are indicated to cover long distances in long-duration fishing trips. Recommended models: NOMAD 7XF, NOMAD 8XF, and TITAN CC.
fishing.p4: Spearfishing: In this modality, the fisherman uses special weapons to shoot fish underwater. Generally, long distances are covered and a lot of space is needed to store all equipment. Recommended models: NOMAD 7CC and NOMAD 7XF.

maintenance.title: Maintenance Routine
maintenance.intro: Your vessel needs some care to always be like new. Establish a routine for checking some fundamental items. Below we suggest a standard:

weekly_items.2: Remove the protective cover to circulate air and take sun.
weekly_items.3: When returning from the sea, flush the outboard motor (flushing is running with fresh water to remove salt residue and prevent accumulation inside the engine).
weekly_items.4: Never store the vessel wet and cover with the tarp. Dry the compartments well. This will prevent it from getting a bad smell and mold inside.
weekly_items.5: Remove the protective cover to circulate air and take sun.

monthly_items.1: Wax the hull and stainless steel hardware.
monthly_items.2: Wash the anchor locker and cables to prevent bad smell.
monthly_items.3: Clean the refrigerator and always leave the door open to prevent mold.

six_months_items.1: Clean the bathroom drains and siphons.
six_months_items.4: Lubricate the canopy zipper with Silicone to prevent jamming.
six_months_items.5: Apply Solid Vaseline on the battery terminal to prevent copper oxidation.
six_months_items.8: Grease the propeller shaft with Blue Marine Grease.

yearly_items.1: Lubricate the trailer bearings and wheels. This will prevent corrosion and seizing.
yearly_items.2: Update the nautical chart with the Port Captaincy.
yearly_items.5: Check the validity of the rockets (pyrotechnics). For NOMAD catamarans, the following are used: 2 manual red star rockets with parachutes, 2 orange floating smoke signals, and 2 manual red light flares.
yearly_items.6: Check the sacrificial anodes located on the outboard motor lower unit.

hydrofoils.title: The Hydrofoil Revolution
hydrofoils.subtitle: First Tests on Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard Catamaran Boats
hydrofoils.p1: Innovation and Technology in the Nautical World: In the current nautical scenario, innovation and technology are crucial elements to advance and improve vessel performance. Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard, known for its tradition and quality, took a significant step by starting the first tests with the use of hydrofoils on its catamaran boats. This technology, already used in racing vessels and high-performance yachts, promises to revolutionize the leisure boat market, bringing considerable benefits in terms of speed, fuel efficiency, and stability.
hydrofoils.p2: What Are Hydrofoils? Hydrofoils are structures similar to submerged wings which, when installed on the hull of a vessel. When the boat reaches a certain speed, these wings create lift, raising the hull and significantly reducing resistance to displacement. This technology drastically reduces friction with water, increasing efficiency and allowing higher speeds with lower fuel consumption. In addition, the use of hydrofoils provides smoother navigation, reducing the impact of waves and offering greater comfort to occupants.
hydrofoils.p3:
First Tests and Results: The first tests carried out by Mastro D'Ascia Shipyard with its catamaran boats equipped with hydrofoils were extremely promising. The collected data reveal:
1. Efficiency Increase and Fuel Reduction up to 26%: The reduction of friction with water allowed a fuel saving of up to 26%, making the vessels more ecological and economical.
2. Top Speed Increase by 3 Knots: Boats equipped with hydrofoils reached a top speed 3 knots above conventional versions, standing out in terms of performance.
3. Greater Stability and Comfort: Navigation became more stable and comfortable, even in rough waters, providing a superior experience for occupants.
hydrofoils.p4: The Future of Catamaran Boats: The success of the first tests opens the way for a new era in Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard catamaran boats. The introduction of hydrofoils not only improves the performance and efficiency of the vessels but also redefines the standard of comfort and sustainability in the nautical segment. With this innovation, the shipyard reaffirms its commitment to excellence and technological vanguard, consolidating its position always aligned with the newest technologies in the market.
hydrofoils.p5: Conclusion: The implementation of hydrofoils in catamaran boats represents a major evolution in the nautical environment. The first tests show a promising future, where speed, efficiency, and comfort go hand in hand, raising the navigation experience to new levels. We are just at the beginning of a journey that promises to transform the way we live the sea, and Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard is at the forefront of this transformation.

safety.title: Safety Tips
safety.intro: Before setting sail, be sure that all procedures and care regarding safety and the proper functioning of the boat have been taken. Check out some tips:
safety.item1: Read and know the COLREGs (International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea) and the Maritime Authority Standards that establish minimum safety requirements for vessels, especially NORMAM 01, NORMAM 02, NORMAM 03 and NORMAM 05.
safety.item2: Perform periodic maintenance of the vessel, without improvisation. Any carelessness could compromise the safety of those on board.
safety.item3: Perform a rigorous inspection of life-saving equipment and utensils. Pay special attention to life jackets, which must be sufficient for everyone boarding. The life jackets used in NOMAD catamarans are CLASS II.
safety.item4: Inspect fire fighting material, pay special attention to the expiration date and conservation status of extinguishers. Indicated extinguishers are ABC powder.
safety.item5: Perform a hull survey, verify the operation of bilge pumps, navigation lights, VHF radio equipment, and battery charge condition, as well as the engine crankcase oil level for 4-stroke engines and oil reservoir level in 2-stroke engines. Also check fuel level integrity, and if there are no leaks in the engine compartment.
safety.item6: Have on board documents proving your qualification, vessel registration, and mandatory insurance within the validity period.
safety.item7: Verify if existing nautical charts on board are compatible with the region where you intend to navigate, pay attention to lighthouses and nautical signaling.
safety.item8: Check if the anchor has enough cable for safe anchoring. Remember that the indicated cable length for safe anchoring is five to seven times the depth of the place where you intend to anchor.
safety.item9: Inform yourself about the weather forecast released by CHM and other agencies, and stay attentive to forecast changes for the period. If everything is fine, plan your voyage.
safety.item10: Deliver the navigation plan or departure notice to the marina or club responsible for guarding the vessel, if not in a club or marina, leave someone on land aware of where you plan to go with the vessel and what time you intend to return.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Mastro D’Ascia, accessible at https://www.mastrodascia.com.br, respects the privacy of its users and is committed to protecting personal data, in compliance with the General Data Protection Law (LGPD – Law No. 13.709/2018).
privacy.desc: This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, and protect information when browsing our site.

section1.title: 1. Information Collection
section1.text: We may collect information voluntarily provided by the user, as well as data collected automatically during navigation, such as IP address, browser type, pages accessed, and time spent on the site.

section2.title: 2. Use of Cookies
section2.text1: We use cookies to ensure the correct functioning of the site, improve your browsing experience, and analyze page performance and usage.
section2.text2: Cookies are small files stored in your browser. You can manage or disable cookies in your browser settings at any time, aware that this may impact some site functionalities.

section3.title: 3. Google Analytics
section3.text1: This site uses Google Analytics, an analysis service provided by Google LLC, which uses cookies to collect anonymous information about how users interact with the site.
section3.text2: The information generated by Google Analytics cookies includes data such as pages visited, time spent, and origin of access, being used exclusively for statistical analysis and improving the user experience.
section3.text3: This data may be stored on servers located outside Brazil, in accordance with Google's privacy practices.

section4.title: 4. Purpose of Data Use
section4.text:
• Ensure site functioning
• Improve browsing experience
• Analyze access and performance metrics
• Comply with legal and regulatory obligations

section5.title: 5. Data Sharing
section5.text: Mastro D’Ascia does not sell, rent, or share personal data with third parties, except when necessary for the operation of the site, compliance with legal obligations, or by judicial determination.

section6.title: 6. Storage and Security
section6.text1: We adopt appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, loss, alteration, or improper disclosure.
section6.text2: Data is stored only for the time necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this policy, unless legal requirements apply.

section7.title: 7. Rights of the Data Subject
section7.text:
• Access to your personal data
• Correction of incomplete or outdated data
• Deletion of data, when applicable
• Revocation of consent

section8.title: 8. Links to Third-Party Sites
section8.text: Our site may contain links to external sites. Mastro D’Ascia is not responsible for the privacy practices or contents of these sites.

section9.title: 9. Changes to this Policy
section9.text: This Privacy Policy may be updated periodically. We recommend that the user reviews this content regularly.

section10.title: 10. Contact
section10.text: In case of doubts about this Privacy Policy or about the treatment of personal data, contact us through the channels available on the site mastrodascia.com.br.
